shootDirection: a high-key white studio caught between takes - mid-conversation, adjusting a jacket, tuning up, relaxed and close to the lens
concept: White cyc studio, between-takes close group portrait
scene: In a high-key white cyc studio late morning, the four selected figures are caught in a close, slightly low-angle black-and-white frame just after a setup break: the long-haired man from 9 stands nearest the lens at left, broad-shouldered and severe in his dark open-collar shirt and heavy pendant, while the cowboy-hatted rocker from 6 sits half-turned on a white apple box beside him, one elbow draped over a raised knee as he adjusts his jacket and glances sideways mid-comment. At center-right, the curly-haired younger man from 4 leans forward from a crouch with clasped hands and an intent but relaxed expression, as if listening before speaking, his face bright in the spill of a large overhead softbox. Behind and slightly deeper in frame, the bespectacled man from 8 stands in a plain dark tee, shoulders easy, looking toward the others rather than the camera, giving the image a real between-takes intimacy. The seamless white floor and wall blow out to bright highlights, but deep blacks in their clothing, hat, sunglasses, hair, and pendant anchor the composition; silver film grain and crisp tonal contrast make every face large, sharp, and tactile without ever feeling like a formal lineup.
prompt: Create one original, photorealistic 16:9 group photograph that brings together the musicians shown across the 4 reference images into a single shared real-world scene. Scene: In a high-key white cyc studio late morning, the four selected figures are caught in a close, slightly low-angle black-and-white frame just after a setup break: the long-haired man from 9 stands nearest the lens at left, broad-shouldered and severe in his dark open-collar shirt and heavy pendant, while the cowboy-hatted rocker from 6 sits half-turned on a white apple box beside him, one elbow draped over a raised knee as he adjusts his jacket and glances sideways mid-comment. At center-right, the curly-haired younger man from 4 leans forward from a crouch with clasped hands and an intent but relaxed expression, as if listening before speaking, his face bright in the spill of a large overhead softbox. Behind and slightly deeper in frame, the bespectacled man from 8 stands in a plain dark tee, shoulders easy, looking toward the others rather than the camera, giving the image a real between-takes intimacy. The seamless white floor and wall blow out to bright highlights, but deep blacks in their clothing, hat, sunglasses, hair, and pendant anchor the composition; silver film grain and crisp tonal contrast make every face large, sharp, and tactile without ever feeling like a formal lineup. Keep every person clearly recognisable and faithful to their reference photo - preserve each face, skin tone, hair, facial hair, glasses, and overall likeness exactly as shown. Do not invent anyone who is not in the reference photos. Keep this an intimate, well-composed group, not a crowd. Where a reference photo shows a band, feature only its single most recognisable member rather than every member. Frame the group tightly - close or medium-close - so every face is large in the frame, sharp, and richly detailed, never small or distant. Arrange them naturally within the scene as one connected group sharing the same moment and light, like a real posed or candid group photo. Vary posture, depth, and where people are looking - this is a caught moment, not a band-publicity lineup evenly spaced and facing the camera. Not separate cut-outs floating side by side, and never a grid, row, or panel of squares. Render photorealistically: real skin texture, true-to-life lighting and depth of field, captured as if shot on a professional camera. This is a real photograph, not an illustration, drawing, painting, cartoon, or render. Colour treatment: black-and-white photography with silver film grain, deep blacks and bright highlights, no colour at all. Mood: relaxed, magnetic, quietly intense. Do not add any extra people beyond the reference subjects. Do not duplicate, merge, blend, or distort faces. Do not include any text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, or signage anywhere in the image. Avoid: readable text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, signage, grid layout, contact sheet, evenly tiled squares, raw album-cover thumbnails, collage of separate panels, illustration, illustrated, drawing, painting, cartoon, comic, anime, vector art, sketch, flat graphic style, extra people, background crowd, duplicated faces, cloned faces, merged faces, blended faces, morphed features, distorted faces, deformed hands, extra limbs. Photorealistic, sharp, high quality, flattering, with real depth and a strong sense of place.
